findings.summary: HM01B0 camera board (46 components, KiCad 9); RT9080 LDO and active oscillator correctly identified; I2C bus missed despite SCL/SDA/CAM_SCL/CAM_SDA nets with R1/R2 pull-ups through JP1 solder jumper

correct.description: Total 46 components correctly identified: 9 resistors, 6 capacitors, 6 jumpers, 4 test points, 4 fiducials, 4 NMOS transistors (Q1-Q4 RE1C002UNTCL), 2 ICs (U1 HM01B0, U2 RT9080), 2 mounting holes, 1 LED, 1 active oscillator (Y1), 3 other. All counts match the schematic.

U2 (RT9080-2.8V) correctly identified as LDO regulator with input_rail=3.3V and output_rail=2.8V. vref_source=fixed_suffix is appropriate since output voltage is encoded in part name. Decoupling capacitors on 2.8V (C8, C2, C7, C5) correctly attributed.

Y1 (24MHz, SparkFun-Clock:Oscillator_24MHz_3.2x2.5mm) correctly classified as active_oscillator. It is a 4-pin package with pins GND/VDD/OUT/EN — an active MEMS/clock oscillator, not a passive crystal. No load_caps reported which is correct: active oscillators have internal load caps.

Q1-Q4 (RE1C002UNTCL NMOS) all 4 correctly detected. Q1 (gate=2.8V, drain=SDA, source=CAM_SDA) and Q2 (gate=2.8V, drain=SCL, source=CAM_SCL) are bidirectional I2C level shifters. Q3/Q4 are camera power/control switches (PWDN, RST). All captured in transistor_circuits.

Power rails correctly identified: 3.3V (host-supplied input), 2.8V (from RT9080 LDO), 1.5V (camera supply rail), GND. All four domains present. Decoupling analysis covers all three voltage rails accurately.

missed.description: I2C bus not detected despite two I2C buses present: SCL/SDA (3.3V host domain, J1/J2/J3 connectors) and CAM_SCL/CAM_SDA (2.8V camera domain, J4 connector) bridged by Q1/Q2 level-shift MOSFETs. R1/R2 (2.2k) are pull-ups for SDA/SCL connected through JP1 (SolderJumper_3_Bridged123, center pin B = 3.3V). bus_analysis.i2c is empty.

I2C pull-ups R1/R2 (2.2k) not recognized because they connect to 3.3V through JP1 solder jumper (SolderJumper_3_Bridged123). The pull-up path is: R1/R2 pin 2 -> unnamed net -> JP1 pin C -> JP1 pin B -> 3.3V. The analyzer does not trace through solder jumpers to find pull-up supply rails.

suggestions: Detect I2C buses by matching net names containing SCL/SDA (case-insensitive) even when prefixed with domain names like CAM_SCL/CAM_SDA
Trace pull-up resistor supply rails through solder jumpers (SolderJumper_* parts) to identify the pull-up voltage, enabling correct pull-up detection for solder-jumper-configurable pull-ups

findings.summary: HM01B0 camera board PCB (4-layer); board outline missing (edge_count=0, bounding_box=null) though board is physically defined; layer stackup, routing, and multi-layer zone fill ratio all correct

correct.description: 4-layer stackup correctly identified: F.Cu, In1.Cu, In2.Cu, B.Cu. copper_layers_used=4 accurate. Stackup shows prepreg-core-prepreg layering with correct KiCad 9 layer numbering (0=F.Cu, 4=In1.Cu, 6=In2.Cu, 2=B.Cu).
33 nets match schematic exactly. routing_complete=true, unrouted_net_count=0 correct. The 4-layer board is fully routed.

GND zone spanning F.Cu, B.Cu, and In1.Cu (3 copper layers) correctly detected as is_filled=true. fill_ratio=2.125 is physically correct: filled_area=1727.43mm2 is the sum of F.Cu (487.71) + B.Cu (532.79) + In1.Cu (706.93) fill areas, against outline_area=812.9mm2. Values > 1 are expected for multi-layer zones.

incorrect.description: Board outline not detected: board_outline.edge_count=0 and bounding_box=null, resulting in board_width_mm=null and board_height_mm=null. The Camera Board is a physically defined PCB but its Edge.Cuts layer data was not parsed. Board area and dimension calculations are unavailable.

suggestions: Investigate why Edge.Cuts arcs or the specific graphic primitives used in this board's outline are not being parsed into the board_outline section
